resources.string: Use the %1$s button to toggle voice recognition, the %2$s button to open this help dialog, and the %3$s menu to change speech settings.
We show arrows for multiple moves when we are not sure. Speak the color or number of a move arrow to select it.

string.name: instructions3
string.comment:
%1$s is "yes", %2$s is "no". Both of these are deliberately in English, which is the only language supported for voice commands.
This is a complicated concept more easy to understand in practice. Voice commands can be misheard and Lichess will not always be fully certain what you said. Several arrows will be shown to identify e.g. if the move was 'b4' or 'd4', which often sound similar. The most likely move the player wants will have a circle showing around it, but it will be possible to cancel before it is played (for example, by shouting 'no!').
You can see a visual example here: https://lichess.org/@/schlawg/blog/how-to-lichess-voice/nWrypoWI

resources.string: If an arrow shows a sweeping radar, that move will be played when the circle is complete. During this time, you may only say %1$s to play the move immediately, %2$s to cancel, or speak the color/number of a different arrow. This timer can be adjusted or turned off in settings.
Enable %s in noisy surroundings. Hold Shift when speaking commands while this option is active.
Use the phonetic alphabet to improve recognition of chessboard files.
%s explains the voice move settings in detail.
Move to e4 or select e4 piece
Cancel timer or deny a request
Play preferred move or confirm something
